tldr: Quiet shipping week. Six items closed since the previous run shipped during the past 7 days (items #10, #11, #18, #19, #20, #24 moved to alreadyShipped). Most of the remaining backlog has been pending for two cycles now: items #12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #17, #21, #22, #23 and #25 — the GBP services audit (#21) and acupuncture-page reframe (#17) are still the highest-leverage Tier-1 fixes that haven't shipped.

Search visibility — second run, 28 queries: Meridian still rank 0 across every tracked query. SERP shape is essentially unchanged from last week (no penalised competitors, no AI-Overview citations of meridianosteopathy.co.nz). One small movement: 'dry needling halswell' now has Elite Physiotherapy's Halswell clinic surfacing — a physio competitor has filled the suburb gap that was uncontested last week. Reinforces urgency of items #17 + suburb signals around acupuncture/dry-needling content.

Two new industry signals worth ship-it items this week: (1) Schema.org v30 (released 2026-03-19) added IndividualPhysician, PhysiciansOffice and the practicesAt property — our Physician schema can be upgraded for sharper entity disambiguation; (2) llms.txt adoption guides now recommend a <link rel='alternate' type='text/markdown'> in the HTML head pointing to /llms.txt — a 1-line head-template change that closes G13. Both added as items #26 and #27.
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images
Site-wide sweep removing 'GP referral' from patient-facing copy (per the CLAUDE.md copy rule); condition CTA banners simplified to heading + booking button only

gaps.title: No condition-specific pages for neck pain, back pain or ACC
gaps.why: Four condition pages now ship (sciatica/headaches/pregnancy/sports), but neck-pain, back-pain and an ACC landing remain — each is the highest-intent variant of an 'osteopath christchurch' query. Confirmed again this run: 0/3 in top 10.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months. April-2026 industry data: 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within 30 days, 50% of Perplexity citations come from content less than 13 weeks old. osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine queries on blog-post velocity.

gaps.title: No llms.txt reference in HTML head
gaps.why: Emerging convention (now consensus best practice in 2026 llms.txt guides): <link rel='alternate' type='text/markdown' href='/llms.txt'> in the head signals AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists. We have /llms.txt, just not the link. New item #26.

gaps.title: Image alt text could be richer
gaps.why: AI-image-grounded models benefit from descriptive, not titular, alt text.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit appears in ~68% of AI-generated responses across major AI platforms in 2026 (replyagent.ai/reddireach data). Item #15 still applies.

gaps.title: Acupuncture page leads with TCM framing
gaps.why: Confirmed by SERP again this week: physios own every 'dry needling christchurch' slot because our acupuncture intro still leads with 'Traditional Chinese Medicine with modern evidence-based practice' (services.json line 52). Item #17 unchanged.

gaps.title: Google Business Profile services incomplete
gaps.why: Google's March 2026 core update made the GBP completeness score a more explicit ranking input. Profiles missing service listings now face a measurable penalty. Item #21 is now a Tier-1 must-do, not a nice-to-have.

gaps.title: Physician schema not using v30 IndividualPhysician + practicesAt
gaps.why: Schema.org v30 (released 2026-03-19) added IndividualPhysician and PhysiciansOffice as Physician subtypes, plus the practicesAt property linking an individual to their MedicalOrganization. Upgrading our team-profile @graph from Physician → IndividualPhysician with practicesAt sharpens entity disambiguation for AI engines. New item #27.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache. Reinforced this week: post-March-2026 Google update weights review recency more heavily than raw count.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Ahrefs analysis of 17M AI citations (April 2026) found AI-cited content is 25.7% fresher than traditional Google organic results — 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within the last 30 days. Freshness compounds.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. Reddit is now Google's #2 visible site (after Wikipedia) and is cited in ~68% of AI-generated responses across ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity (April 2026). 90/10 rule: 90% non-promotional, 2–4 weeks of karma-building before any mention of Meridian.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old. Reinforced 2026-05-02: Google's March 2026 core update now weights review recency AND owner response rate more heavily than raw count. Healthcare best-practice = at least 1 new review/week.

items.title: Reframe acupuncture page as Western medical / dry needling first
items.body: Surfaced by a real-world ChatGPT test: the user searched 'Western acupuncture / dry needling near Halswell' and ChatGPT routed them to physio clinics first because our acupuncture page leads with 'Traditional Chinese Medicine with modern evidence-based practice'. Flip the ordering: lead with 'Medical acupuncture / dry needling — Western evidence-based technique for trigger-point and musculoskeletal pain', then mention TCM as a complementary framework. Name Nina's Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) in the first paragraph — it's the exact credential ChatGPT told users to look for. 2026-05-02 confirmed: services.json line 52 still leads with TCM, and every top-10 slot for 'dry needling christchurch' is still a physio clinic (Muscle People, Fixed Physio, Physiosouth, Koru, Health and Sport Central). 'dry needling halswell' now has Elite Physiotherapy's Halswell clinic appearing for the first time — the suburb gap is closing without us.

items.title: Audit and expand Google Business Profile services
items.body: ChatGPT pulled the clinic as 'Meridian Osteopathy - Medical Acupuncture Clinic' straight from GBP. Whatever services are listed on GBP becomes what AI tools can cite. Log into Google Business Profile and add these services: Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine. ~10 minutes of clicking. Cannot be shipped by the Ship-it button — owner task. PROMOTED to Tier 1 this run: Google's March 2026 core update made GBP completeness score a more explicit ranking input, with profiles missing service listings 'facing a measurable penalty' (multiple 2026 healthcare-SEO sources).

items.title: Not ranking for 'neck pain osteopath christchurch' — betterhealthosteopathy.nz owns it
items.body: betterhealthosteopathy.nz and osteopath.nz both have dedicated neck-pain condition URLs that win this query; Meridian is not in the top 10 (confirmed again 2026-05-02). This is NOT covered by item #10's four-page scope (sciatica / headaches / pregnancy / sports) — those four shipped but neck pain didn't. Add a 5th condition page — 800–1200 words Q→A with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema — mirroring the item #10 pattern. Internal-link from the osteopathy service page.

items.title: Not ranking for 'back pain osteopath christchurch' — betterhealthosteopathy.nz owns it
items.body: Better Health's /back-pain-relief-osteopath-christchurch/ is the #1 result for this query cluster (confirmed again 2026-05-02). Back pain is probably the single highest-intent query osteopathy patients search — it must have its own page. Mirror the item #10 pattern: MedicalCondition + FAQPage, cross-link to ACC info and the osteopathy service page, include a section on acute vs chronic and when to seek care.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query (confirmed again 2026-05-02). We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, how to lodge a claim, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.

items.title: Add <link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown"> in HTML head
items.body: G13 (no llms.txt reference in head) is now addressable with a single-line head template change. The 2026 llms.txt guides (BigCloudy, ALLMO, AEO Press) all converge on this convention: a <link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown" href="/llms.txt"> in <head> signals to AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists alongside the HTML, even though most still discover it via /llms.txt directly. Optionally also add the same link with href="/llms-full.txt" and a title attribute. Trivial change, no risk to CSP.

items.title: Upgrade Physician schema to v30 IndividualPhysician with practicesAt
items.body: Schema.org v30 (released 2026-03-19) introduced two new Physician subtypes: IndividualPhysician (a person who practises medicine) and PhysiciansOffice (the place they practise at). The new practicesAt property on IndividualPhysician points to the MedicalOrganization where the individual practises — exactly the relationship we currently express through worksFor / employee in the @graph. Switching @type from Physician to IndividualPhysician on each team-profile node and adding practicesAt: { @id: '#clinic' } should be a small JSON-LD edit in the team-profile partial. No content changes, no visible UI changes, no CSP impact. Sharpens entity disambiguation for ChatGPT / Perplexity / AI Overviews that map our team to the clinic graph.
